# Exchange - Full Documentation

# Administrator Tasks

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Exchange administrators have permissions to perform these tasks:

- [Discover and catalog external agents](discovering-and-importing-external-agents)

  Use agent scanners to automatically discover and catalog agents from outside Anypoint Platform.

- [Importing Agentforce Agents](importing-agentforce-agents)

  Import Agentforce agents into Exchange to include them in business groups.

- [Share an asset](to-share-an-asset)

  Share an asset to publish an API asset to a public portal, or to grant access to an asset to
  specific users in your organization, to everyone in your organization, or to everyone in a chosen
  external organization.

- [Publish an API to a public portal](to-share-api-asset-to-portal)

  Publish APIs to a public portal for external users.

- [Saving Searches](to-save-searches)

  Save and maintain searches for assets within a business group.

- [Use API Manager to add the Request Access button](to-enable-the-request-access-button)

  Specify which API assets can be shared with users.

- [Set User Access Permissions](to-set-permissions)

  Grant the Exchange Administrator, Contributor, Viewer, or Creator permissions to members of your
  organization, and optionally provide access for individual assets.

- [About Exchange cookies](cookies)

  Find information about Exchange cookies, cookie configuration, and cookies for vanity domains.

- [Migrate Exchange 1 assets to Exchange 2](migrate)

  Move assets from Exchange 1.
---

# Consuming an API

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Anypoint Exchange provides significant features for API providers to easily publish and share APIs
with developers inside and outside of an organization.

Exchange helps API consumers understand API functionality and test features with:

- API console to view API functions. This console appears to the right of a REST API detail screen.
- A mocking service to simulate data to an API.
- GraphQL console to view GraphQL schemas and run queries.

## See Also

- [View REST API Elements in an Asset](asset-details#view-rest-api-elements-in-an-asset)
- [Simulating Data in REST APIs](ex2-to-simulate-api-data)
- [Viewing GraphQL API Schemas and Run Queries](view-graphql-api-schemas-and-run-queries)
---

# Documenting an API

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

To increase usability and adoption, create documentation using Einstein generative AI, document APIs
using API Notebook, and configure API instances.

- [Generating API Documentation with Generative AI](generating-documentation-with-ai)

  Create API documentation by using Einstein generative AI.

- [Documenting an Asset Using API Notebook](to-use-api-notebook)

  > [!NOTE] This feature of the product has reached
  > [End of Life](https://www.salesforce.com/en-us/wp-content/uploads/sites/4/documents/legal/Agreements/versioning-back-support-policy.pdf).

  Create API Notebooks to document each function in your API and provide a test area so that users
  can experiment with sending different values to the API and receiving output.

  After creating your API Notebook, create an API instance to control whether the notebook is
  private or public.

## See Also

- [Editing an Asset](to-describe-an-asset)
---

# Manage Client Applications

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Manage your client applications by using the **My Applications** page. This page lists applications
that hold a contract with an API or an API group. To view this page, select **My applications** from
the navigation bar.

![ex2 my applications](_images/ex2-my-applications.png)

Applications are registered using API Manager in Anypoint Platform.

To register a client application to an existing API or API Group, the client application must first
request access. When the request is approved by the API or API Group owner, a contract is created
between the client application and the API or API Group, and the client application is registered.

If you donât have an application to link to the API or API Group, you can create the application in
the **Request Access** menu. You can then click **Request Access** to connect your application and
the API or API Group. Some APIs and API Groups require selecting a service-level agreement (SLA),
and some APIs and API Groups require accepting terms and conditions.

APIs and API Groups that are protected by a client ID enforcement policy require client applications
to provide a client ID and optional client secret. The client ID and client secret credentials are
automatically created when the client application is registered.

An API owner can use API Manager to view all API contracts ever created for an API or API Group
instance. Client app owners can assign other users and teams to view and manage a client application
using the **My Applications** page in Exchange.

An API consumer can use the Exchange **My Applications** page to view the contracts created between
an API instance or API Group instance and that consumerâs client applications.

## View Client Applications Details

Each client application has a client ID, client secret, description, URL, redirect URIs, grant
types, and usage dashboard.

To see the details of a client application, select **My application** from the sidebar and select an
application from the list.

To find an application, search the list of applications by entering a name or description in
**Search** and press **Enter**.

## Edit Client Application Details

You can change the client application details. These include name, description, URL, and OAuth 2.0
redirect URIs.

To make changes, click **Edit**. After making your updates, click **Update** to save your changes.

## Delete a Client Application

When you donât need the contract for an application, you can delete it. After the application is
deleted, thereâs no longer a contract with the application and you canât access it. To regain
access, request access to the API again.

1.  From the sidebar, select **My applications** and click the name of the application.
2.  Click ![The more options icon](_images/ex2-more-options-icon.png) and select **Delete
    application**.
3.  Confirm that you want to delete the application by clicking **Delete application**.

## Reset Client Secret

You can reset the client secret for an application on a set schedule to ensure security, even if
users leave the company. Resetting the secret also ensures it isnât unintentionally shared with
unauthorized users.

Applications using the current client secret canât access any of the APIs with which they were
registered.

1.  From the sidebar, select **My applications** and click the name of the application.
2.  Click ![The more options icon](_images/ex2-more-options-icon.png) and select **Reset client
    secret**.
3.  Confirm that you want to delete the application by clicking **Reset client secret**.

    The client secret is reset. In the application details section, click **Show** to see the new
    client secret.

## Add and Change an SLA Tier

SLA tiers are defined in API Manager. If an application doesnât have an SLA tier listed, you can
request to add an SLA tier. You can also change the SLA tier for an application. The available SLA
tiers are set by an admin in API Manager. The owner of the API instance must approve the request to
add or change the tier level.

To add or change an SLA tier:

1.  From the sidebar in Exchange, select **My applications** and click the name of the application.
2.  From the instances panel, move the cursor over an instance and click **View SLA Tier**.
3.  Click **Manage SLA Tiers**, select an SLA tier, and click **Submit**.

## View Usage Information

See usage information for the instances of a client application using the application dashboard. The
dashboard shows graphs for:

- Total requests
- Average latency (ms)
- Requests by status code
- Requests by HTTP status code
- Error percentage

You can set a date range to display for all the charts in your dashboard. If not specified, the
range is the previous eight days.

1.  From the sidebar in Exchange, select **My applications** and click the name of the application.
2.  Select one of more instance.
3.  Click the calendar dropdown, select a date range, and click **Confirm**.

## See Also

- [Request Access](to-request-access)
---

# Exchange Platform APIs

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Anypoint Exchange provides several APIs to help you manage assets:

- **Exchange API**: Publish and consume assets, and retrieve metadata for Agent Fabric assets.
- **Exchange Maven Facade API**: Publish and consume assets.
- **Exchange Graph API**: Search for assets.

The Maven facade also enables you to publish federated assets.

Exchange APIs:

- [Exchange API](https://anypoint.mulesoft.com/exchange/portals/anypoint-platform/f1e97bc6-315a-4490-82a7-23abe036327a.anypoint-platform/exchange-experience-api/)
- [Exchange Maven Facade API](https://anypoint.mulesoft.com/exchange/portals/anypoint-platform/f1e97bc6-315a-4490-82a7-23abe036327a.anypoint-platform/exchange-maven-facade-api-http/)
- [Exchange Graph API](https://anypoint.mulesoft.com/exchange/portals/anypoint-platform/f1e97bc6-315a-4490-82a7-23abe036327a.anypoint-platform/exchange-graph-http/)

Topics:

- [Publishing Assets Using the Exchange API](exchange-api)
- [Publishing Assets Using Maven](to-publish-assets-maven)
- [Searching the Graph API](to-search-with-graph-api)
- [Retrieving Asset Metadata Using an MCP Server Endpoint](retrieve-asset-metadata-using-mcp)

## See Also

- [Anypoint Platform APIs - Developer Portal](https://anypoint.mulesoft.com/exchange/portals/anypoint-platform/)
- [Consuming an API](about-api-use)
- [Configuring Instances](to-configure-api-settings)
---

# Exchange Public Portals

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Exchange public portals are accessible to anyone using the internet, enabling developers outside of
an organization to access that organizationâs REST, SOAP, and HTTP APIs. Users who are not logged in
can download assets from public portals.

Exchange public portals replace what used to be known as _developer portals_.

You can customize the appearance and the content of the portal by adding a logo, banner image, text,
or a favicon for the browser tab.

## Share an Asset or Update the Asset Description

As an Exchange administrator, you can edit the description of an asset in your organization so that
users outside the organization can identify it, and then share the asset by publishing to a public
portal:

1.  Locate the asset in your organizationâs Exchange instance.
2.  Edit the asset description and then publish the asset.
3.  Verify the change by returning to the public portal and clicking the asset.

## How Users from Other Organizations Access Your Portal

Anyone can view a public portal without logging in.

Anypoint users can click the **Login** button on any page to use features such as reviewing assets,
downloading assets, creating a client application to request API access, and accessing an API.

Anypoint users who belong to other Anypoint organizations and not your organization are _external
users_ of your organizationâs portal.

An organization administrator can see only the basic profile of an external user.

A basic profile has only these fields:

- **Name**
- **Username**
- **Email**
- **Primary Organization**

The first time an external user attempts to log in to your public portal, the portal requests
permission for read-only access to this basic profile. When the user approves this request, they are
logged in to your public portal and their information is added to the **External Users** tab. To
view this tab in Anypoint Platform click **Access Management** > **Users** > **External Users**.

As your organizationâs administrator, you can enable or disable each external user on that tab.
Users are enabled by default. If you disable them, they cannot log in to your public portal.

Users in your organization can access features that external users cannot, such as deploying an
application. You can invite an external user to join your organization so they can use these
features.

## See Also

- [Customize an Exchange Public Portal](to-customize-portal)
- [Public Portal Vanity Domain](portal-vanity-domain)
---

# Publishing Assets

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Publish APIs, API Groups, templates, connectors, policies, Mule XML example code, and custom
information assets to a private Exchange instance or an Exchange public portal. Exchange provides a
repository for code and APIs, and helps your organization access them easily, model them, and reuse
them.

A private Exchange instance makes it easy to provide assets to internal developers by giving them
viewer, contributor, or administrator access. Additionally, assets created within a business group
are automatically provided to the developers of that business group.

An Exchange public portal enables Exchange administrators to provide assets to developers outside of
their own organizations.

Both Exchange views also let users review assets so that asset providers can get feedback.

See the [FAQ](exchange2-faq) for additional information.
---

# Adding Policy Implementation Files to Published Policy Definition

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

When a policy definition asset is published in Exchange, the asset details page displays a
**Pending** flag next to **Implementations** in the left navigation bar. The flag indicates that the
policy implementation has not been added. Add the policy implementation files to the policy
definition to enable the policy to be available in runtime. The required implementation files are a
binary file such as a WebAssembly (WASM) file for Omni Gateway or JAR for Mule 4 runtime and a YAML
metadata file.

Before adding the policy implementation files, ensure that the policy definition asset is in the
Stable state.

To add the policy implementation files:

1.  In Exchange, select the policy asset to open the asset details page.
2.  Click **Implementations** in the left navigation bar.
3.  Click **Add implementation**.
4.  Enter the name for the implementation.
5.  Click **Choose file** to select the JAR or WebAssembly file.
6.  Click **Choose file** to select the YAML file.
7.  To edit the GroupId, AssetId, Version, and API version, click **Advanced**.

    Exchange generates the group ID, asset ID, and version. You can change these values. You can
    also change an API assetâs version and API version separately. The advanced settings are most
    often used to change the asset version.

8.  Click **Add implementation**.

    The implementation displays in the **Manage implementations** table. From this table, manage the
    implementation versions of a policy asset. See [Manage Versions](manage-versions) for more
    information.
---

# Adding a Scanner for Amazon Bedrock AgentCore MCP

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Add a scanner to discover, import, and sync MCP servers from Amazon Bedrock AgentCore MCP Gateway
into Exchange. Then you can govern the MCP servers and consume them in other applications.

## Before You Begin

Before adding the scanner, verify that you have the permission, context, and these Amazon AgentCore
credentials:

- An active AWS account to register and host MCP servers
- Amazon access key ID
- Amazon secret access key
- Amazon region
- Exchange Administrator permission
- An IAM user with an inline policy that allows these permissions:
  - bedrock-agentcore:ListAgentRuntimes
  - bedrock-agentcore:GetAgentRuntime
  - bedrock-agentcore:ListAgentRuntimeVersions
  - bedrock-agentcore:ListAgentRuntimeEndpoints
  - bedrock-agentcore:InvokeAgentRuntime

For more information, see the
[Amazon Bedrock documentation](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock-agentcore/).

## Add a Scanner for Amazon AgentCore

1.  Verify that you are in the business group where you want to add the scanner.
2.  From the sidebar in Exchange, click **Scanners**.
3.  Enter a name for the scanner.
4.  In **Scanner Run Configuration**, complete these fields or options:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th><strong>Field/Option</strong></th><th><strong>Value</strong></th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Run Schedule</strong></p></td><td><p>Select a frequency and local time.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Sync Review</strong></p></td><td><p>Select an option: Auto-resolve or Ask to review.</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

5.  From **Connection Configuration**, complete these fields:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th><strong>Field</strong></th><th><strong>Value</strong></th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Provider</strong></p></td><td><p>Select <strong>Amazon</strong>.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Platform</strong></p></td><td><p>Select <strong>AWS AgentCore</strong>.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Service Type</strong></p></td><td><p>Select <strong>MCPs</strong>.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Authentication Method</strong></p></td><td><p>Select <strong>Access key</strong>.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Access Key ID</strong></p></td><td><p>Enter the access key ID.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Secret Access Key</strong></p></td><td><p>Enter the secret access key.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>AWS Region</strong></p></td><td><p>Select a region.</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

6.  Click **Test Connection**.

    If the connection fails, review the **Connection Configuration** settings. Update the settings,
    and then test the connection again.

7.  To send email notifications:
    1.  Select **Advanced Settings** and turn on **Send Email Notifications**.
    2.  Enter an email address.

8.  Click **Add Scanner**.

## See Also

- [Amazon scanners](aws-scanners)
- [Discovering and Cataloging External Assets with Scanners](discovering-and-importing-external-agents)
- [MCP scanners](mcp-scanners)
- [Managing Scanners](managing-scanners)
---

# Adding a Scanner for Amazon Bedrock

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Add a scanner to discover, import, and sync agents from Amazon Bedrock into Exchange. Then you can
govern the agents and consume them in other applications.

## Before You Begin

Before adding the scanner, verify that you have the permission, context, and these Amazon Bedrock
credentials:

- Exchange Administrator permission
- Knowledge about the Amazon Web Services region where the agents are deployed.
- Amazon Bedrock access key ID
- Amazon Bedrock secret access key
- Ensure you have these Amazon Bedrock permissions:
  - bedrock:ListAgents
  - bedrock:GetAgent
  - bedrock:ListAgentAliases
  - bedrock:GetAgentAlias
  - bedrock:ListAgentVersions
  - bedrock:GetAgentVersion

- To make sure the scanner discovers the agent, assign an alias and link that alias to a version.
  The scanner ignores agents without an invocable URL.

## Add a Scanner for Amazon Bedrock

1.  Verify that you are in the business group where you want to add the scanner.
2.  From the sidebar in Exchange, click **Scanners**.
3.  Enter a name for the scanner.
4.  From **Scanner Run Configuration**, complete these fields or options:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th><strong>Field/Option</strong></th><th><strong>Value</strong></th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Run Schedule</strong></p></td><td><p>Select a frequency and time.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Sync Review</strong></p></td><td><p>Select Auto-resolve.</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

5.  From **Connection Configuration**, complete these fields:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th><strong>Field</strong></th><th><strong>Value</strong></th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Provider</strong></p></td><td><p>Select <strong>Amazon</strong>.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Platform</strong></p></td><td><p>Select <strong>AWS Bedrock</strong>.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Authentication Method</strong></p></td><td><p>Select <strong>Access key</strong>.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Access Key ID</strong></p></td><td><p>Enter the access key ID.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Secret Access Key</strong></p></td><td><p>Enter the secret access key.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>AWS Region</strong></p></td><td><p>Select a region.</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

6.  Click **Test Connection**.

    If the connection fails, review the **Connection Configuration** settings. Update the settings,
    and then test the connection again.

7.  To send email notifications:
    1.  Select **Advanced Settings** and turn on **Send Email Notifications**.
    2.  Enter an email address.

8.  Click **Add Scanner**.

## See Also

- [Amazon Scanners](aws-scanners)
- [Discovering and Cataloging External Services with Scanners](discovering-and-importing-external-agents)
- [Managing Scanners In Exchange](managing-scanners)
---

# Adding a Scanner for Amazon API Gateway

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Add a scanner to discover, import, and sync APIs from Amazon API Gateway into Anypoint Exchange.
Once imported, you can apply governance rulesets and publish them to your developer portal.

## Before You Begin

Before adding the scanner, verify that you have the permission, context, and these Amazon API
Gateway credentials:

- Exchange Administrator permission
- IAM policy with read-only permissions for Amazon API Gateway
- Knowledge about the Amazon Web Services region where the services are deployed
- AWS access key ID
- AWS secret access key

Example custom IAM policy for read-only access:

```json
{
  "Version": "2012-10-17",
  "Statement": [
    {
      "Effect": "Allow",
      "Action": ["apigateway:GET"],
      "Resource": "*"
    }
  ]
}
```

## Add a Scanner for Amazon API Gateway

1.  Verify that you are in the business group where you want to add the scanner.
2.  From the sidebar in Exchange, click **Scanners**.
3.  Enter a name for the scanner.
4.  From **Scanner Run Configuration**, complete these fields or options:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th><strong>Field/Option</strong></th><th><strong>Value</strong></th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Run Schedule</strong></p></td><td><p>Select a frequency and local time.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Sync Review</strong></p></td><td><p>Select an option: Auto-resolve or Ask to review.</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

5.  From **Connection Configuration**, complete these fields:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th><strong>Field</strong></th><th><strong>Value</strong></th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Provider</strong></p></td><td><p>Select <strong>Amazon</strong>.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Platform</strong></p></td><td><p>Select <strong>API Gateway</strong>.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Service Type</strong></p></td><td><p><strong>REST APIs</strong> selected by default.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Authentication Method</strong></p></td><td><p><strong>Access key</strong> selected by default.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>AWS Access Key ID</strong></p></td><td><p>Enter the access key ID.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>AWS Secret Access Key</strong></p></td><td><p>Enter the secret access key.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>AWS Region</strong></p></td><td><p>Select a region.</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

6.  Click **Test Connection**.

    If the connection fails, review the **Connection Configuration** settings. Update the settings,
    and then test the connection again.

7.  To send email notifications:
    1.  Select **Advanced Settings** and turn on **Send Email Notifications**.
    2.  Enter an email address.

8.  Click **Add Scanner**.

## See Also

- [AWS scanners](aws-scanners)
- [Discovering and Cataloging External Assets with Scanners](discovering-and-importing-external-agents)
- [Managing Scanners](managing-scanners)
---

# Adding a Scanner for Google Apigee

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Add a scanner to discover, import, and sync APIs from Google Apigee into Anypoint Exchange. Once
imported, you can apply governance rulesets and publish them to your developer portal.

## Before You Begin

Before adding the scanner, verify that you have the permission, context, and these Google Apigee
credentials:

- Exchange Administrator permission
- Apigee Read-only Admin permission
- Google Cloud Platform (GCP) project ID
- Service account email
- Private key

## Add a Scanner for Google Apigee

1.  Verify that you are in the business group where you want to add the scanner.
2.  From the sidebar in Exchange, click **Scanners**.
3.  Enter a name for the scanner.
4.  From **Scanner Run Configuration**, complete these fields or options:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th><strong>Field/Option</strong></th><th><strong>Value</strong></th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Run Schedule</strong></p></td><td><p>Select a frequency and local time.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Sync Review</strong></p></td><td><p>Select an option: Auto-resolve or Ask to review.</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

5.  From **Connection Configuration**, complete these fields:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th><strong>Field</strong></th><th><strong>Value</strong></th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Provider</strong></p></td><td><p>Select <strong>Google</strong>.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Platform</strong></p></td><td><p>Select <strong>Google Apigee</strong>.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Service Type</strong></p></td><td><p><strong>REST APIs</strong> selected by default.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Authentication Method</strong></p></td><td><p><strong>Service Account</strong> selected by default.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>GCP Project ID</strong></p></td><td><p>Enter the Google Cloud Platform project ID.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Service Account Email</strong></p></td><td><p>Enter the service account email.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Private Key</strong></p></td><td><p>Enter the private key.</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

6.  Click **Test Connection**.

    If the connection fails, review the **Connection Configuration** settings. Update the settings,
    and then test the connection again.

7.  To send email notifications:
    1.  Select **Advanced Settings** and turn on **Send Email Notifications**.
    2.  Enter an email address.

8.  Click **Add Scanner**.

## See Also

- [Google scanners](google-scanners)
- [Discovering and Cataloging External Assets with Scanners](discovering-and-importing-external-agents)
- [Managing Scanners](managing-scanners)
---

# Adding a Scanner for Azure API Management

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Add a scanner to discover, import, and sync APIs from Azure API Management into Anypoint Exchange.
Once imported, you can apply governance rulesets and publish them to your developer portal.

## Before You Begin

Before adding the scanner, verify that you have the permission, context, and these Azure API
Management credentials:

- Exchange Administrator permission
- API Management Service Reader Role permission
- Tenant ID
- Client ID
- Client Secret
- Subscription ID
- Resource Group
- Service Name

## Add a Scanner for Azure API Management

1.  Verify that you are in the business group where you want to add the scanner.
2.  From the sidebar in Exchange, click **Scanners**.
3.  Enter a name for the scanner.
4.  From **Scanner Run Configuration**, complete these fields or options:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th><strong>Field/Option</strong></th><th><strong>Value</strong></th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Run Schedule</strong></p></td><td><p>Select a frequency and local time.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Sync Review</strong></p></td><td><p>Select an option: Auto-resolve or Ask to review.</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

5.  From **Connection Configuration**, complete these fields:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th><strong>Field</strong></th><th><strong>Value</strong></th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Provider</strong></p></td><td><p>Select <strong>Microsoft</strong>.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Platform</strong></p></td><td><p>Select <strong>Azure API Management</strong>.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Service Type</strong></p></td><td><p><strong>REST APIs</strong> selected by default.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Authentication Method</strong></p></td><td><p><strong>OAuth</strong> selected by default.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Tenant ID</strong></p></td><td><p>Enter the tenant ID.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Client ID</strong></p></td><td><p>Enter the client id.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Client Secret</strong></p></td><td><p>Enter the client secret.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Subscription ID</strong></p></td><td><p>Enter the subscription ID.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Resource Group</strong></p></td><td><p>Enter the resource group.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Service Name</strong></p></td><td><p>Enter the service name.</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

6.  Click **Test Connection**.

    If the connection fails, review the **Connection Configuration** settings. Update the settings,
    and then test the connection again.

7.  To send email notifications:
    1.  Select **Advanced Settings** and turn on **Send Email Notifications**.
    2.  Enter an email address.

8.  Click **Add Scanner**.

## See Also

- [Microsoft scanners](microsoft-scanners)
- [Discovering and Cataloging External Assets with Scanners](discovering-and-importing-external-agents)
- [Managing Scanners](managing-scanners)
---

# Adding a Scanner for Amazon Bedrock AgentCore Runtime

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Add a scanner to discover, import, and sync agents from Agent Runtime into Exchange. Then you can
govern the agents and consume them in other applications.

## Before You Begin

This setup requires specific configurations and access privileges in Amazon Bedrock to enable the
scanner to access and list Amazon Bedrock AgentCore agents.

- Prerequisites:
  - An active AWS account to register and host agents.
  - Access to Amazon Bedrock AgentCore in the AWS Console.
  - An AWS app registration in the AWS Console to generate credentials.

- Required Configuration Values:
  - **AWS Region** for endpoint URL construction (for example,
    `[https://bedrock.<region>.amazonaws.com](https://bedrock.\<region\>.amazonaws.com)`).
  - **Agent Endpoint and Version** because created agents must have an assigned endpoint and version
    to be discoverable.
  - **Access Key and Secret Access Key** generated for the Identity and Access Management (IAM)
    user.

- Required Permissions and Roles:
  - An IAM user with an inline policy that allows:
    - `bedrock-agentcore:ListAgentRuntimes`
    - `bedrock-agentcore:ListAgentRuntimeEndpoints`
    - `bedrock-agentcore:GetAgentCard`
    - `bedrock-agentcore:GetAgentRuntime`
    - `bedrock-agentcore:ListAgentRuntimeVersions`

  - Standard Bedrock permissions are also required:
    - `bedrock:GetAgent`
    - `bedrock:ListAgents`
    - `bedrock:InvokeAgent` (for AgentCore agents)

- Required Authentication:
  - IAM Signature (SigV4) is the default authentication method for AWS users.
  - OAuth 2.0 is available for AgentCore through `CreateOauth2CredentialProvider`.

- Scanner Configuration Scope and Limitations:
  - Agents must be published with an active endpoint for scanner discovery.
  - Security reviews indicate medium server-side request forgery (SSRF) risk if a user-supplied
    region is not validated during URL construction.
  - Basic AgentCore agents do not expose A2A cards, but AgentCore supports A2A cards when agents
    expose them.

For more information, see the
[Amazon Bedrock documentation](https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock-agentcore/).

## Add a Scanner for Amazon Bedrock AgentCore

1.  Verify that you are in the business group where you want to add the scanner.
2.  From the sidebar in Exchange, click **Scanners**.
3.  Enter a name for the scanner.
4.  In **Scanner Run Configuration**, complete these fields or options:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th><strong>Field/Option</strong></th><th><strong>Value</strong></th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Run Schedule</strong></p></td><td><p>Select a frequency and local time.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Sync Review</strong></p></td><td><p>Select an option: Auto-resolve or Ask to review.</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

5.  From **Connection Configuration**, complete these fields:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th><strong>Field</strong></th><th><strong>Value</strong></th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Provider</strong></p></td><td><p>Select <strong>Amazon</strong>.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Platform</strong></p></td><td><p>Select <strong>AWS AgentCore</strong>.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Asset Type</strong></p></td><td><p>Only agents are supported.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Authentication Method</strong></p></td><td><p>Select <strong>Access key</strong>.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Access Key ID</strong></p></td><td><p>Enter the access key ID.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Secret Access Key</strong></p></td><td><p>Enter the secret access key.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>AWS Region</strong></p></td><td><p>Select a region.</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

6.  Click **Test Connection**.

    If the connection fails, review the **Connection Configuration** settings. Update the settings,
    and then test the connection again.

7.  To send email notifications:
    1.  Select **Advanced Settings** and turn on **Send Email Notifications**.
    2.  Enter an email address.

8.  Click **Add Scanner**.

## See Also

- [Amazon Scanners](aws-scanners)
- [Discovering and Cataloging External Services with Scanners](discovering-and-importing-external-agents)
- [Managing Scanners In Exchange](managing-scanners)
---

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th><strong>Field/Option</strong></th><th><strong>Value</strong></th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Run Schedule</strong></p></td><td><p>Select a frequency and time.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Sync Review</strong></p></td><td><p>Select an option: <strong>Auto-resolve</strong> or <strong>Ask to review</strong>.</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

5.  From **Connection Configuration**, complete these fields:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th><strong>Field</strong></th><th><strong>Value</strong></th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Provider</strong></p></td><td><p>Select <strong>Anthropic</strong>.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Platform</strong></p></td><td><p>Select <strong>Anthropic Claude</strong>.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Service Type</strong></p></td><td><p><strong>Agents</strong> selected by default.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Authentication Method</strong></p></td><td><p>Select <strong>Access Keys</strong>.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>API Key</strong></p></td><td><p>Enter the API key.</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

6.  Click **Test Connection**.

    If the connection fails, review the **Connection Configuration** settings. Update the settings,
    and then test the connection again.

    1.  Select **Advanced Settings** and turn on **Send Email Notifications**.
    2.  Enter an email address.

# Adding a Scanner for Databricks Agent Bricks

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Add a scanner to discover, import, and sync agents from Databricks Agent Bricks into Exchange. Then
you can govern the agents and consume them in other applications.

## Before You Begin

Before adding the scanner, verify that you have these:

- Exchange Administrator permission
- Databricks Workspace access
- Workspace URL
- Databricks Client ID
- Databricks Client secret
- Service Principal requires CAN_QUERY permission on each serving endpoint to enable full discovery
  and invocation.

  Use the Databricks Permissions API:

  ```json
  PATCH /api/2.0/permissions/serving-endpoints/{endpoint_id}
  {
    "access_control_list": [
      {
        "service_principal_name": "<clientId>",
        "permission_level": "CAN_QUERY"
      }
    ]
  }
  ```

  <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>API Endpoint</th><th>Required Permission</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>GET /api/2.0/serving-endpoints</p></td><td><p>CAN_VIEW or higher</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>GET /api/2.0/serving-endpoints/{name}</p></td><td><p>CAN_VIEW or higher</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>GET /api/2.0/serving-endpoints/{name}/openapi</p></td><td><p>CAN_VIEW or higher</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>POST /serving-endpoints/{name}/invocations</p></td><td><p>CAN_QUERY or higher</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Add a Scanner for Databricks Agent Bricks

1.  Verify that you are in the business group where you want to add the scanner.
2.  From the sidebar in Exchange, click **Scanners**.
3.  Enter a name for the scanner.
4.  From **Scanner Run Configuration**, complete these fields or options:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th><strong>Field/Option</strong></th><th><strong>Value</strong></th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Run Schedule</strong></p></td><td><p>Select a frequency and time.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Sync Review</strong></p></td><td><p>Select an option: <strong>Auto-resolve</strong> or <strong>Ask to review</strong>.</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

5.  From **Connection Configuration**, complete these fields:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th><strong>Field</strong></th><th><strong>Value</strong></th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Provider</strong></p></td><td><p>Select <strong>Databricks</strong>.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Platform</strong></p></td><td><p>Select <strong>Agent Bricks</strong>.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Service Type</strong></p></td><td><p>Select <strong>Agents</strong> selected by default.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Authentication Method</strong></p></td><td><p><strong>OAuth</strong> selected by default.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Workspace URL</strong></p></td><td><p>Enter the workspace URL.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Client ID</strong></p></td><td><p>Enter the client ID.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Client Secret</strong></p></td><td><p>Enter the client secret.</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

6.  Click **Test Connection**.

    If the connection fails, review the **Connection Configuration** settings. Update the settings,
    and then test the connection again.

7.  To send email notifications:
    1.  Select **Advanced Settings** and turn on **Send Email Notifications**.
    2.  Enter an email address.

8.  Click **Add Scanner**.

## See Also

- [Agent Scanners](agent-scanners)
- [Discovering and Cataloging External Services with Scanners](discovering-and-importing-external-agents)
- [Managing Scanners In Exchange](managing-scanners)

# Adding a Scanner for GoDaddy ANS

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Add a scanner to discover, import, and sync agents from GoDaddy ANS into Exchange. Then you can
govern the agents and consume them in other applications.

GoDaddy agents are verified by GoDaddy to be safe and secure.

## Before You Begin

Before adding the scanner, verify that you have the permission and these GoDaddy ANS credentials:

- Exchange Administrator permission
- GoDaddy ANS API key
- GoDaddy ANS API secret

  You enter your GoDaddy ANS credentials when configuring the scanner in Exchange. For more
  information, see the GoDaddy ANS documentation.

## Add a Scanner for GoDaddy ANS

1.  Verify that you are in the business group where you want to add the scanner.
2.  From the sidebar in Exchange, click **Scanners**.
3.  Enter a name for the scanner.
4.  From **Scanner Run Configuration**, complete these fields or options:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th><strong>Field/Option</strong></th><th><strong>Value</strong></th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Run Schedule</strong></p></td><td><p>Select a frequency and time.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Sync Review</strong></p></td><td><p>Select Auto-resolve.</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

5.  From **Connection Configuration**, complete these fields:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th><strong>Field</strong></th><th><strong>Value</strong></th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Agent Provider</strong></p></td><td><p>Select <strong>GoDaddy ANS</strong>.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Authentication Method</strong></p></td><td><p>Select <strong>SSO Key</strong>.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>API Key</strong></p></td><td><p>Enter the API key.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>API Secret</strong></p></td><td><p>Enter the API secret.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Environment</strong></p></td><td><p>Select an environment.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Agent Display Name Filter</strong></p></td><td><p>Enter a name to partially match and include specific agents.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Agent FQDN Filter</strong></p></td><td><p>Enter a fully qualified domain name (FQDN) for an exact match to include specific agents. For example, <code>agent.example.com</code>.</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

6.  Click **Test Connection**.

    If the connection fails, review the **Connection Configuration** settings. Update the settings,
    and then test the connection again.

7.  To send email notifications:
    1.  Select **Advanced Settings** and turn on **Send Email Notifications**.
    2.  Enter an email address.

8.  Click **Add Scanner**.

## See Also

- [GoDaddy scanners](godaddy-scanners)
- [Discovering and Cataloging External Assets with Scanners](discovering-and-importing-external-agents)
- [Managing Scanners](managing-scanners)

govern the agents and consume them in other applications.

credentials:

- Exchange Administrator permission
- Vertex AI Viewer permission
- Google Vertex AI project ID
- Google Vertex AI secret access key

  You enter these Google Vertex AI credentials when configuring the scanner in Exchange. For more
  information about creating a service account key, see Google Vertex AI documentation.

## Add a Scanner for Google Vertex AI

2.  From the sidebar in Exchange, click **Scanners**.
3.  Enter a name for the scanner.
4.  From **Scanner Run Configuration**, complete these fields or options:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th><strong>Field/Option</strong></th><th><strong>Value</strong></th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Run Schedule</strong></p></td><td><p>Select a frequency and local time.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Sync Review</strong></p></td><td><p>Select an option: Auto-resolve or Ask to review.</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th><strong>Field</strong></th><th><strong>Value</strong></th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Agent Provider</strong></p></td><td><p>Select <strong>Google Vertex AI</strong>.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Authentication Method</strong></p></td><td><p>Select <strong>Service Account</strong>.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Account Email</strong></p></td><td><p>Enter the email address.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Project ID</strong></p></td><td><p>Enter the project ID.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Secret Access Key</strong></p></td><td><p>Enter the access key.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Location</strong></p></td><td><p>Select a location.</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

    If the connection fails, review the **Connection Configuration** settings. Update the settings,

    2.  Enter an email address.

# Adding a Scanner for Kong Gateway

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Add a scanner to discover, import, and sync APIs from Kong Gateway into Anypoint Exchange. Once
imported, you can apply governance rulesets and publish them to your developer portal.

## Before You Begin

Before adding the scanner, verify that you have the permission, context, and these Kong credentials:

- Exchange Administrator permission \*Kong Control Plane Viewer permission
- Kong Gateway region
- Personal access token (PAT)

## Add a Scanner for Kong Gateway

1.  Verify that you are in the business group where you want to add the scanner.
2.  From the sidebar in Exchange, click **Scanners**.
3.  Enter a name for the scanner.
4.  From **Scanner Run Configuration**, complete these fields or options:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th><strong>Field/Option</strong></th><th><strong>Value</strong></th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Run Schedule</strong></p></td><td><p>Select a frequency and local time.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Sync Review</strong></p></td><td><p>Select an option: Auto-resolve or Ask to review.</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

5.  From **Connection Configuration**, complete these fields:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th><strong>Field</strong></th><th><strong>Value</strong></th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Provider</strong></p></td><td><p>Select <strong>Kong</strong>.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Platform</strong></p></td><td><p>Select <strong>Kong Gateway</strong>.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Service Type</strong></p></td><td><p><strong>REST APIs</strong> selected by default.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Authentication Method</strong></p></td><td><p><strong>Service Account</strong> selected by default.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Kong Gateway Region</strong></p></td><td><p>Select the Kong Gateway region.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Personal Access Token</strong></p></td><td><p>Enter the personal access token.</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

6.  Click **Test Connection**.

    If the connection fails, review the **Connection Configuration** settings. Update the settings,
    and then test the connection again.

7.  To send email notifications:
    1.  Select **Advanced Settings** and turn on **Send Email Notifications**.
    2.  Enter an email address.

8.  Click **Add Scanner**.

# Adding a Scanner for LangChain LangSmith

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Add a scanner to discover, import, and sync agents from LangSmith into Exchange. Then you can govern
the agents and consume them in other applications.

## Before You Begin

Before adding the scanner, verify that you have these:

- Exchange Administrator permission
- Plus plan (or higher) workspace in LangSmith
- LangSmith API key for the workspace
- LangSmith workspace ID

## Add a Scanner for LangChain LangSmith

1.  Verify that you are in the business group where you want to add the scanner.
2.  From the sidebar in Exchange, click **Scanners**.
3.  Enter a name for the scanner.
4.  From **Scanner Run Configuration**, complete these fields or options:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th><strong>Field/Option</strong></th><th><strong>Value</strong></th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Run Schedule</strong></p></td><td><p>Select a frequency and time.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Sync Review</strong></p></td><td><p>Select an option: <strong>Auto-resolve</strong> or <strong>Ask to review</strong>.</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

5.  From **Connection Configuration**, complete these fields:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th><strong>Field</strong></th><th><strong>Value</strong></th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Provider</strong></p></td><td><p>Select <strong>LangChain</strong>.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Platform</strong></p></td><td><p>Select <strong>LangSmith</strong>.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Service Type</strong></p></td><td><p><strong>Agents</strong> is selected by default.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Authentication Method</strong></p></td><td><p><strong>Access Keys</strong> selected by default.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>LangSmith API Key</strong></p></td><td><p>Enter the API key.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Workspace ID</strong></p></td><td><p>Enter the workspace ID.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Region</strong></p></td><td><p>Select the region for your workspace: US or EU. Default is the US region.</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

6.  Click **Test Connection**.

    If the connection fails, review the **Connection Configuration** settings. Update the settings,
    and then test the connection again.

7.  To send email notifications:
    1.  Select **Advanced Settings** and turn on **Send Email Notifications**.
    2.  Enter an email address.

8.  Click **Add Scanner**.

- [Discovering and Cataloging External Services with Scanners](discovering-and-importing-external-agents)

# Adding a Scanner for Microsoft Azure Copilot

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Add a scanner to discover, import, and sync agents from Microsoft Azure Copilot into Exchange. Then
you can govern the agents and consume them in other applications.

## Before You Begin

This setup requires specific configurations and access privileges in Microsoft Copilot Studio and
Power Platform so the scanner can access and list agents.

- Prerequisites
  - An App Registration must be completed in the Azure Portal to generate necessary credentials.

- Required Configuration Values:
  - Power Platform tenant ID
  - Power Platform client ID (Application ID)
  - Power Platform client secret

- Required Permissions and Roles:

  Add the registered application as an Application User in Power Platform admin center and assign it
  the Copilot Studio Scanner Role security role.

- Required Scope:

  Set the scope to Dataverse Environment URL,
  `[https://<org-Id>.crm.dynamics.com](https://\<org-Id\>.crm.dynamics.com)`.

## High-level Steps to Configure Microsoft Copilot Studio

To configure Microsoft Copilot Studio before you add a scanner, review these high-level steps. For
details, see the
[Microsoft Copilot Studio documentation](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-copilot-studio/).

1.  Create an app registration:
    1.  Create a new app registration, select the account types, and register the application.
    2.  Create client secrets and copy the values.
    3.  Record these configuration values from the registered application. These values are required
        when adding a scanner in Exchange:
        - Client ID (Application ID)
        - Client Secret
        - Tenant ID (Directory ID)

2.  Configure permissions:
    1.  In Power Platform, go to **Environments** and select the environment.
    2.  Create a custom security role with read-only permission to the `bot` and `botcomponents`
        tables:
        1.  Go to **Security roles** and add a new role.
        2.  Add or select these values in the **Create New Role** panel, and then save:

            <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th><strong>Field</strong></th><th><strong>Value</strong></th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Role Name</strong></p></td><td><p>Copilot Studio Scanner Role</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Business Unit</strong></p></td><td><p>Select the business unit.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Description</strong></p></td><td><p>Read-only access to scan Copilot Studio agent metadata.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Applies To</strong></p></td><td><p>Copilot Studio scanning and auditing.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Summary of Core Table Privileges</strong></p></td><td><p>Read access to the <code>bot</code> and <code>botcomponents</code> tables.</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

        3.  From the Details page, enter `bot` to locate the Agent and Agent Component table
            records.
        4.  Select **Organization Access** from the **Read** column for both Agent and Agent
            Component, and then save.

    3.  In Power Platform admin center, create a new app user by selecting **Application users** and
        adding the app you created.
    4.  Select the **Business Unit** and edit the **Security Role** by selecting the **Copilot
        Studio Scanner Role** that you created.

3.  After you complete these steps, add the scanner in Exchange.

## Add a Scanner for Microsoft Copilot Studio

1.  Verify that you are in the business group where you want to add the scanner.
2.  From the sidebar in Exchange, click **Scanners**.
3.  Enter a name for the scanner.
4.  From **Scanner Run Configuration**, complete these fields or options:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th><strong>Field/Option</strong></th><th><strong>Value</strong></th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Run Schedule</strong></p></td><td><p>Select a frequency and time.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Sync Review</strong></p></td><td><p>Select <strong>Auto-resolve</strong>.</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

5.  From **Connection Configuration**, complete these fields:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th><strong>Field</strong></th><th><strong>Value</strong></th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Agent Provider</strong></p></td><td><p>Select <strong>Microsoft Azure Copilot</strong>.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Authentication Method</strong></p></td><td><p>Select an option.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Tenant ID</strong></p></td><td><p>Enter the tenant ID.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Client ID</strong></p></td><td><p>Enter the client ID.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Client Secret</strong></p></td><td><p>Enter the client secret.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Scope</strong></p></td><td><p>Enter the URL.</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

6.  Click **Test Connection**.

    If the connection fails, review the **Connection Configuration** settings. Update the settings,
    and then test the connection again.

7.  To send email notifications:
    1.  Select **Advanced Settings** and turn on **Send Email Notifications**.
    2.  Enter an email address.

8.  Click **Add Scanner**.

## See Also

- [Microsoft scanners](microsoft-scanners)
- [Discovering and Cataloging External Assets with Scanners](discovering-and-importing-external-agents)
- [Managing Scanners](managing-scanners)
---

# Adding a Scanner for Microsoft Foundry

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Add a scanner to discover, import, and sync agents from Microsoft Foundry into Exchange. Then you
can govern the agents and consume them in other applications.

## Before You Begin

This setup requires specific configurations and access privileges within the Azure environment to
enable the agent scanner to access and list Microsoft Foundry agents.

- Prerequisites
  - An Active Azure Subscription is required for creating a Microsoft Foundry project.
  - An Azure App Registration must be completed in the Azure portal to generate necessary
    credentials in the Azure portal.
  - A new project created using the resource type, Microsoft Foundry resource.

- Required Configuration Values

  These values, which are obtained during the Azure App registration setup, are required for scanner
  configuration:
  - Tenant ID (Directory ID)
  - Client ID (Application ID)
  - Client Secret

- Required Permissions and Roles:
  - The application principal from the prerequisite step must be assigned the Azure AI Developer
    role within the Azure portal to grant the scanner access.

- Scanner Configuration Limitations:

  Agent listing is project-specific:
  - The user must provide all corresponding Project IDs during scanner configuration if agents are
    spread across multiple projects.
  - A new scanner configuration is required for every new project created.

  For more information, see the
  [Microsoft Foundry documentation](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/ai-foundry/).

## Add a Scanner for Microsoft Foundry

1.  Verify that you are in the business group where you want to add the scanner.
2.  From the sidebar in Exchange, click **Scanners**.
3.  Enter a name for the scanner.
4.  In **Scanner Run Configuration**, complete these fields or options:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th><strong>Field/Option</strong></th><th><strong>Value</strong></th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Run Schedule</strong></p></td><td><p>Select a frequency and local time.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Sync Review</strong></p></td><td><p>Select an option: Auto-resolve or Ask to review.</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

5.  From **Connection Configuration**, complete these fields:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th><strong>Field</strong></th><th><strong>Value</strong></th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Provider</strong></p></td><td><p>Select <strong>Azure AI Foundry</strong>.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Asset Type</strong></p></td><td><p>Only agents are supported.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Authentication Method</strong></p></td><td><p>Select <strong>OAuth 2 Client Credentials</strong>.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Tenant ID</strong></p></td><td><p>Enter the application ID.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Client ID</strong></p></td><td><p>Enter the directory ID.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Client Secret</strong></p></td><td><p>Enter the client secret.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Project Endpoints</strong></p></td><td><p>Enter the project endpoint URLs separated by commas.</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

6.  Click **Test Connection**.

    If the connection fails, review the **Connection Configuration** settings. Update the settings,
    and then test the connection again.

7.  To send email notifications:
    1.  Select **Advanced Settings** and turn on **Send Email Notifications**.
    2.  Enter an email address.

8.  Click **Add Scanner**.

## See Also

- [Microsoft scanners](microsoft-scanners)
- [Discovering and Cataloging External Assets with Scanners](discovering-and-importing-external-agents)
- [Managing Scanners](managing-scanners)
---

# Adding a Scanner for Snowflake Cortex AI

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Add a scanner to discover, import, and sync agents from Snowflake Cortex AI into Exchange. Then you
can govern the agents and consume them in other applications.

## Before You Begin

Before adding the scanner, verify that you have these:

- Exchange Administrator permission
- Snowflake Enterprise edition account with Cortex agents enabled
- Snowflake ACCOUNTADMIN role permission
- Snowflake account URL
- Snowflake programmatic access token (PAT)

## Add a Scanner for Snowflake Cortex AI

1.  Verify that you are in the business group where you want to add the scanner.
2.  From the sidebar in Exchange, click **Scanners**.
3.  Enter a name for the scanner.
4.  From **Scanner Run Configuration**, complete these fields or options:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th><strong>Field/Option</strong></th><th><strong>Value</strong></th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Run Schedule</strong></p></td><td><p>Select a frequency and time.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Sync Review</strong></p></td><td><p>Select an option: <strong>Auto-resolve</strong> or <strong>Ask to review</strong>.</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

5.  From **Connection Configuration**, complete these fields:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th><strong>Field</strong></th><th><strong>Value</strong></th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Provider</strong></p></td><td><p>Select <strong>Snowflake</strong>.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Platform</strong></p></td><td><p>Select <strong>Cortex AI</strong>.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Service Type</strong></p></td><td><p><strong>Agents</strong> is selected by default.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Authentication Method</strong></p></td><td><p><strong>Service Account</strong> selected by default.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Account URL</strong></p></td><td><p>Enter the account URL.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Programmatic Access Token</strong></p></td><td><p>Enter the programmatic access token.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Database Filter</strong></p></td><td><p>To filter by database, enter the database name, or a comma-separated list of database names.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Schema Filter</strong></p></td><td><p>To filter by schema, enter the schema name, or a comma-separated list of schema names.</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

6.  Click **Test Connection**.

    If the connection fails, review the **Connection Configuration** settings. Update the settings,
    and then test the connection again.

7.  To send email notifications:
    1.  Select **Advanced Settings** and turn on **Send Email Notifications**.
    2.  Enter an email address.

8.  Click **Add Scanner**.

## See Also

- [Amazon Scanners](aws-scanners)
- [Discovering and Cataloging External Services with Scanners](discovering-and-importing-external-agents)
- [Managing Scanners In Exchange](managing-scanners)

# Adding a Scanner for Snowflake MCP Server

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

Add a scanner to discover, import, and sync MCP servers from Snowflake MCP Server into Exchange.
Then you can govern the servers and consume them in other applications.

## Before You Begin

Before adding the scanner, verify that you have these:

- Exchange Administrator permission
- Snowflake ACCOUNTADMIN role permission
- Snowflake Enterprise edition account with MCP servers enabled
- Snowflake account URL
- Snowflake programmatic access token (PAT)

## Add a Scanner for Snowflake MCP

1.  Verify that you are in the business group where you want to add the scanner.
2.  From the sidebar in Exchange, click **Scanners**.
3.  Enter a name for the scanner.
4.  From **Scanner Run Configuration**, complete these fields or options:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th><strong>Field/Option</strong></th><th><strong>Value</strong></th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Run Schedule</strong></p></td><td><p>Select a frequency and time.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Sync Review</strong></p></td><td><p>Select an option: <strong>Auto-resolve</strong> or <strong>Ask to review</strong>.</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

5.  From **Connection Configuration**, complete these fields:

    <table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th><strong>Field</strong></th><th><strong>Value</strong></th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Provider</strong></p></td><td><p>Select <strong>Snowflake</strong>.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Platform</strong></p></td><td><p>Select <strong>Snowflake MCP Server</strong>.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Service Type</strong></p></td><td><p>Select <strong>MCPs</strong>.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Authentication Method</strong></p></td><td><p><strong>Service Account</strong> selected by default.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Account URL</strong></p></td><td><p>Enter the Snowflake account URL.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Programmatic Access Token</strong></p></td><td><p>Enter the programmatic access token.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Database Filter</strong></p></td><td><p>To filter by database, enter the database name, or a comma-separated list of database names.</p></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Schema Filter</strong></p></td><td><p>To filter by schema, enter the schema name, or a comma-separated list of schema names.</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

6.  Click **Test Connection**.

    If the connection fails, review the **Connection Configuration** settings. Update the settings,
    and then test the connection again.

7.  To send email notifications:
    1.  Select **Advanced Settings** and turn on **Send Email Notifications**.
    2.  Enter an email address.

8.  Click **Add Scanner**.

catalog agents into Exchange so you can govern them and use them from other applications.

- [Adding a Scanner for Amazon Bedrock](adding-amazon-bedrock-scanner)

- Exchange Administrator permission
- Verify that you are in the business group where you want to add the scanner.

# API Scanners

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

API scanners connect external API gateways to Anypoint Platform, enabling you to discover, import,
and sync APIs from external platforms directly into Anypoint Exchange. Once synced, you can enforce
governance policies across all connected gateways.

These are the supported API gateway scanners:

- [Amazon API Gateway](adding-amazon-gateway-scanner)
- [Azure API Management](adding-azure-api-management-scanner)
- [Google Apigee API Gateway](adding-apigee-scanner)
- [Kong API Gateway](adding-kong-gateway-scanner)

- Exchange Administrator permission
- Verify that you are in the business group where you want to add the scanner.
